notes: All three prompts resolve to the real open issue #7 (AI title generation for sessions) in NexWan/agent-web-chat, each via a different input modality (number / title-search / URL). Runs are executed dry-run: no branch creation, no gh writes, no push on the real repo — agents emit the proposed branch name, git commands, and comment body as files instead. Future iterations can add a bug-typed issue and an error/not-found case.

evals.name: feature-by-number
evals.expected_output: Resolves issue #7 from the current repo; reads body + comments; explores backend/frontend to locate where session title generation would live; asks a few specific clarifying questions (e.g. when titles regenerate, which model, sync vs async); proposes a feat/issue-7-<slug> branch off origin/main; produces a concrete plan (problem, scope, approach, files, steps, testing, risks). Does not implement, does not post/push in the dry run.

evals.name: feature-by-title-search
evals.prompt: there's a github issue about auto-generating titles for chat sessions with AI — can you pick it up? get me a branch and a plan.
evals.expected_output: Uses gh issue list --search to find the matching issue (#7) since no number was given, confirms which issue it picked, then proceeds the same as eval-0: read, scope, clarifying questions, feat/ branch off origin/main, full plan. Resolution-by-keywords is the key thing tested here.

evals.name: feature-by-url
evals.expected_output: Parses owner/repo/number from the URL, confirms it matches the current repo, then reads + scopes + asks questions + proposes a feat/ branch + plan as above. URL parsing and the current-repo match check are the key things tested.
